description: This schematic diagram illustrates the dislocation pile-up mechanism near grain boundaries in plastically deformed metallic materials, specifically SPCE/SPCC low-carbon steel sheets. Panel (a) depicts dislocations (represented by black squares on dashed slip planes) moving under applied shear stress τ toward the grain boundary. Panel (b) shows the resulting dislocation pile-up configuration at the grain boundary, where dislocations accumulate and become trapped. The diagram visualizes the spatial distribution of mobile dislocations, demonstrating higher dislocation density near grain boundaries compared to grain interiors. This model explains why the apparent elastic modulus decreases near grain boundaries—mobile dislocations released from Cottrell atmospheres during plastic deformation pile up at boundaries and move back during unloading, reducing the local elastic modulus. The figure supports the core hypothesis that heterogeneous dislocation distribution causes position-dependent elastic properties, with lower modulus values measured near grain boundaries than in grain centers, as subsequently verified by nano-indentation tests.
